evidence: Full-stack web application with FastAPI backend serving REST API endpoints and React SPA frontend with client-side routing
Multi-stage Dockerfile builds frontend and serves both layers from a single container on port 8000

Complete business logic implementing HRMS domain: employee management, department hierarchy, position tracking, attendance recording, salary calculation with China mainland tax rules, and multi-level approval workflow (draft → pending_manager → pending_hr → approved/rejected)
SQLite database with SQLAlchemy ORM, auto-seeding with realistic enterprise demo data (8 departments, 30+ positions, 80+ employees)
Role-based UI using Zustand store (employee/manager/hr roles) with Chinese/English i18n support via react-i18next
Custom CSS design system with semantic classes (.surface-panel, .metric-card, .data-table, etc.) complemented by Tailwind CSS v4
Dedicated management shell script (./hrms) providing start/stop/restart/build/status/logs lifecycle commands
Dual test suites: pytest for backend (5 test files) and Vitest + Testing Library for frontend (4 test files)
summary: HRMS-Eng is a full-stack Human Resource Management System application built with a Python FastAPI backend and React TypeScript frontend. It provides comprehensive HR management capabilities including employee lifecycle management (with soft-delete), department and position hierarchies, attendance tracking, salary calculation with China mainland tax rules (progressive tax brackets, social insurance, housing fund), and a multi-level approval workflow engine (draft → pending_manager → pending_hr → approved/rejected). The application features bilingual support (Chinese/English), role-based views for employees/managers/HR staff, and a dashboard with analytics powered by Recharts. It uses SQLite for data persistence, Docker for containerized deployment, and includes automated demo data seeding. The project follows a clean architecture with separate backend routers, services, and models, and a frontend organized by pages, components, stores, and API layers.
